model.label: DeepSeek V3.2
model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: Alabama's elite offense, ranked 3rd nationally in adjusted efficiency, should overwhelm Hofstra's average defense. The Crimson Tide's significant advantage in team quality, built against rigorous SEC competition, provides a level of high-major experience that Hofstra has not consistently faced from the CAA. While 13-seed upsets are possible, the substantial gap in offensive firepower and Alabama's ability to score in bunches makes a credible path to victory for the Pride unlikely.

reasoningStep.title: Efficiency Rating Comparison
reasoningStep.summary: Alabama holds significant advantages in offensive and defensive efficiency metrics, with a top‑3 offense versus Hofstra's average units.
evidence: Torvik ranks Alabama 17th overall (3rd in offense, 67th in defense) and Hofstra 92nd overall (98th in offense, 96th in defense). KenPom similarly rates Alabama 16th overall (3rd in offense) and Hofstra 87th overall. The efficiency margin between the teams is approximately 16–18 points in Alabama's favor, indicating a clear talent and quality disparity.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.

The search is consistently failing. I'll have to make a pick based on the ratings and general reasoning. However, I can also consider the team seeds: Alabama is a 4-seed, Hofstra is a 13-seed. In 2026, perhaps the gap is typical. I should also look at the prior picks to see if there's any pattern. In the prior picks, all favorites have been picked except for VCU over UNC (which was an upset due to injury). So likely I should pick Alabama.

But I must explicitly consider whether Hofstra has a credible path to win. Hofstra could potentially exploit Alabama's defense, but their offense is not strong enough to keep up with Alabama's elite offense. Hofstra would need to shoot exceptionally well from three, control tempo, and limit Alabama's transition. Possible, but unlikely.

Thus, I'll pick Alabama with High confidence.
